saida_bruta:
“Your face seems familiar,” he said politely. “Weren’t you in the First Division during the war?”

“Why, yes. I was in the Twenty-eighth Infantry.”

“I was in the Sixteenth until June of nineteen-eighteen. I knew I’d seen you somewhere before.”

We spoke briefly about a few damp, grey villages in France. He apparently lived nearby, for he mentioned he’d just bought a hydroplane and planned to try it out the next morning.

“Care to join me, old sport? Just along the shore of the Sound.”

“What time?”

“Whenever suits you best.”

I was about to ask his name when Jordan glanced over and smiled.

“Enjoying yourself now?” she asked.

“Much more so.” I turned back to my new acquaintance. “This is a strange party for me—I haven’t even met the host. I live just over there,” I said, gesturing toward the distant hedge, “and this man Gatsby sent his chauffeur with an invitation.”

He looked at me for a moment as though he didn’t quite understand.

“I’m Gatsby,” he said abruptly.

“What!” I exclaimed. “Oh, forgive me.”

“I assumed you knew, old sport. I’m afraid I make a poor host.”

We talked for a while about a few damp, gray little villages in France. He evidently lived nearby, for he mentioned that he'd just bought a hydroplane and meant to try it out in the morning.

"Want to come along, old sport? Just a little run along the Sound."

"What time?"

"Whenever suits you best."

I was about to ask his name when Jordan looked over and smiled.

"Having a better time now?" she asked.

"Much better." I turned back to my new acquaintance. "This is a strange party for me—I haven't even met the host. I live right over there," I said, waving toward the distant hedge, "and this man Gatsby sent his chauffeur with an invitation."

For a moment he looked at me as if he didn't understand.

"I'm Gatsby," he said suddenly.

"What!" I exclaimed. "Oh, forgive me."

"I thought you knew, old sport. I'm afraid I'm not a very good host."
